'''Tebru''' was a rocky [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Toria Tell's Droid Journal" /> that was located within the [[Tebru system/Legends|Tebru system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">{{JournalCite|10|Toria Tell's Droid Journal (article)}} mentions the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in an excerpt from an [[in-universe]] [[Toria Tell's Droid Journal (scandoc)|scandoc]] published by [[Toria Tell]], implying that she was active in the [[Time/Legends|time]] following that conflict. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the Battle of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] inventor and [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Toria Tell]] maintained a large residence with a workshop and a massive [[Library/Legends|library]] on the planet. Tell kept her extensive droid collection, which contained many of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] oldest and rarest robots, at her home. When not traveling off-world aboard her stock [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] in order to expand her droid collection, the inventor quietly tinkered at her Tebru home.<ref name="Toria Tell's Droid Journal" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tebru was first mentioned in "[[Toria Tell's Droid Journal (article)|Toria Tell's Droid Journal]]," an article written by [[Christian Piccolo]] and published in [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 10]] in [[May]] [[1996]].<ref name="Toria Tell's Droid Journal" /> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Tebru [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Tebru, in grid square P-17.<ref name="EAOC" />
